files.covers: Engine.Providers named registry — register, get, remove, setDefault, list, sub-engine inheritance + pool return cleanup + fallback with local overlay
ICache.TryAddAsync atomic semantics for nonce replay prevention
Ed25519Provider — key generation, sign, verify at provider level
Sign action handler — field population, contracts, TTL, headers, crypto validity, provider override, errors
Verify action handler — all 9 error keys, contracts, provider resolution, headers, nonce cache, boundaries, empty contracts, lazy verification (DESIGN-1), different nonce not-false-positive
Deterministic JSON serialization — null-signature pattern, camelCase, stable bytes, base64, round-trip, Verified JsonIgnore exclusion, invalid base64 rejection
Provider module actions — load, remove, setDefault, list
Identity create delegates to IKeyProvider — mock provider, default Ed25519, error paths

files.tests: sign with 1s TTL, wait, verify fails as expired
verify fails when Created older than timeout setting
tamper data after signing, verify fails with hash mismatch
verify same nonce twice, second fails
sign with headers, verify with matching headers
sign with POST header, verify expects GET, fails
access %data.Signature.Verified% without explicit verify step — DESIGN-1 lazy verification
explicit provider param, default verification, non-existent provider error
verify data that was never signed, should error
sign and verify empty/minimal data object

design_decisions.title: Lazy verification on %data.Signature.Verified% access
design_decisions.description: Accessing %data.Signature.Verified% MUST trigger verification lazily — the PLang developer should NOT need to call an explicit 'verify' step first. The 'verify' action exists for when you need to pass contracts or headers, but bare property access to .Verified must do the verification automatically on first access.
design_decisions.implementation: SignedData.Verified needs a lazy resolution pattern (like IdentityData). First access triggers the full verification flow, caches the result, returns it. Extract the verify handler's core logic into a shared method that both the verify action and the lazy .Verified getter can call. The lazy path uses default contracts (['C0']) and no expected headers. The explicit verify action passes developer-specified contracts and headers.

design_decisions.title: ProviderModuleTests lives under Modules/provider/ not Modules/signing/
design_decisions.description: The provider module (load, remove, setDefault, list) is a separate module from signing per the architect plan. Test file is at PLang.Tests/Runtime2/Modules/provider/ProviderModuleTests.cs — matches the module structure at PLang/Runtime2/modules/provider/.

what_exists.description: All test stubs and PLang test goals are committed on runtime2-builder-v2-signing branch. C# stubs use Assert.Fail('stub — ...') pattern with arrange/act/assert comments. PLang goals are complete — they will work once the module is registered and the builder can parse sign/verify steps.
what_exists.patterns_to_follow: Identity module (piece 1) is the reference. See PLang.Tests/Runtime2/Modules/identity/ for test setup (PLangEngine, Before/After(Test), Ctx property). See PLang/Runtime2/modules/identity/create.cs for handler structure ([Action], IContext, Data.FromError for errors, never throw).

what_coder_must_do: Implement all files listed in the architect plan (origin/runtime2-builder-v2-signing branch)
Make all 85 C# test stubs pass — replace Assert.Fail with real assertions once handlers exist
Make all 14 PLang test goals pass once module is registered
Handlers must NEVER throw — every exception path returns Data.FromError()
Implement lazy verification on SignedData.Verified (DESIGN-1 above) — shared logic with verify action
Verify action's core logic must be reusable by the lazy .Verified getter
summary: 95 C# test stubs + 16 PLang test goals committed for signing module (piece 3). All 9 verify error keys covered. Provider registry (18 tests incl. pool-return cleanup + parent fallback with local overlay), cache atomics, serialization (9 tests incl. invalid base64 rejection), identity key delegation all have dedicated test files. Verify handler has not-false-positive nonce test. CRITICAL: implement lazy verification on SignedData.Verified — accessing the property must trigger verification without an explicit verify step. The verify action and lazy getter share the same underlying logic. Lazy verification covered by 4 C# tests + 1 PLang test. Verified JsonIgnore exclusion tested. Empty contracts list rejection tested. PLang provider swap test now also covers non-existent provider error path.
